parameters.content:

This workflow uses GraphQL calls to the Shopify Admin API. In order to get a better understanding for the queries and mutations please check the API Docs.


[Shopify GraphQL API docs]({{ $env.API_BASE_URL }}

To make it easy to build queries for the GraphQL API easy please check out the [GraphiQL App for the Admin API]({{ $env.API_BASE_URL }} from Shopify

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Every day at 00:00".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Baserow" node (n8n-nodes-base.baserow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "No Operation, do nothing" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get orders from Shopify" node (n8n-nodes-base.graphql) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Shopify data into n8n items" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Check if "Campaign" is present" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set Shopify Subdomain".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Transform incoming data structure".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
